result.chapter_summary: Chapter 2 critiques developmentalism as an individualistic educational doctrine that replaced adult-guided instruction with a faith in the 'providential' nature of a child's instincts. Hirsch argues this philosophy contradicts modern brain science and sociobiology, which show that human nature is a mixture of selfish and altruistic impulses requiring social direction.

claims.text: Current American individualism, which prioritizes the 'I' over the 'WE,' has been fostered by the individualistic doctrines of developmentalism.
claims.source_passage: My point will be that the current individualism in America which elevates the “I” over the “WE” has been helped along by the individualistic doctrines of developmentalism which completely took over our elementary education by mid-twentieth century.

claims.text: Developmentalism completely took over American elementary education by the mid-twentieth century.
claims.source_passage: the individualistic doctrines of developmentalism which completely took over our elementary education by mid-twentieth century.

claims.text: Classroom furniture currently used in schools is designed for child-centered education and is poorly suited for teacher-led instruction.
claims.source_passage: Even if a school decided to introduce a definite curriculum that is teacher-led rather than child-centered, we would still need to use this furniture. Currently, that’s a little awkward.

claims.text: Underlying developmentalism is an unstated religious faith in the providential rightness of nature.
claims.source_passage: Behind that principle is another, unstated one: The child’s instincts and powers come from nature, and nature is the agent of God’s providence.

claims.text: The earlier American educational view held that a child's instincts require adult content and guidance.
claims.source_passage: Our earlier view (just check those earlier American schoolbooks) had been that the child’s instincts and powers are a mixed affair which need to be given content and guidance.

claims.text: Developmentalism assumes that natural growth is both ethically and psychologically the optimal mode of education.
claims.source_passage: Underlying our child-centered education is the implicit assumption that natural growth – natural development – is the optimal mode of education – both ethically and psychologically.

claims.text: Modern developmental psychology and brain science indicate that child instincts are not purely benign but include conflicting impulses.
claims.source_passage: But the latest work in developmental psychology and brain science paints a more complex, less benign picture of the child’s inborn instincts and powers.

claims.text: Humans are born with a biological inheritance of both selfish individualism and empathetic altruism.
claims.source_passage: That universal biological inheritance persists in the human brain – the selfish instinct and the group-centered, unselfish instinct.

claims.text: It is the role of schooling to influence the balance between a child's selfish and unselfish instincts.
claims.source_passage: Both are inborn, and it is up to human schooling to influence how they comport themselves within the child, and within the wider culture.

claims.text: Current science and foundational American traditions both dissent from the contemporary faith in natural development.
claims.source_passage: All this is relevant to our current educational policy because current science – and our earlier American traditions, which built the nation – dissent from our current faith in natural development.

claims.text: The American education system ranks twenty-fifth in the world because it honors unformed natural instincts over social direction.
claims.source_passage: Do we want to continue having the twenty-fifth best elementary school system in the world, because we want to continue assuming that the child’s natural instincts and impulses are providential and are to be honored and followed when in fact they are an unformed mixture of selfishness and altruism mixed uncertainly?

claims.text: Hyper-individualism (prioritizing 'I' over 'WE') has recently dominated in America and weakened national cohesion.
claims.source_passage: hyper-individualism (the “I” over the “WE”) has recently dominated in America and has weakened our cohesion.

claims.text: The American elementary school system is currently the twenty-fifth best in the world.
claims.source_passage: Do we want to continue having the twenty-fifth best elementary school system in the world

claims.text: A child's natural instincts and impulses are an unformed mixture of selfishness and altruism rather than being inherently providential.
claims.source_passage: the child’s natural instincts and impulses are providential and are to be honored and followed when in fact they are an unformed mixture of selfishness and altruism mixed uncertainly

claims.text: Left to chance, selfish individualism is probable to dominate human behavior.
claims.source_passage: Left to chance, it’s probable that selfish individualism will dominate

claims.text: Developmental education has been a primary cause of the decline in American literacy scores.
claims.source_passage: Developmental education has been a cause of our downswing in literacy scores!

claims.text: Literacy depends on shared background knowledge, which constitutes a functional 'shared ethnicity.'
claims.source_passage: literacy depends on shared background knowledge – shared ethnicity.

claims.text: The educational assumption that children learn reading through non-existent 'general skills' using diverse or uncoordinated materials leads to declining literacy.
claims.source_passage: When children are assumed to be learning non-existent general skills by means of diverse self-selected reading materials, or uncoordinated materials selected by individual teachers, children’s literacy will decline

claims.text: Schools that teach the communicative store of shared knowledge see dramatic rises in reading scores.
claims.source_passage: When, by contrast, schools teach the communicative store of shared knowledge that constitutes an ethnicity, reading scores rise dramatically

claims.text: Developmentalism must be removed from the school system starting in kindergarten.
claims.source_passage: For that to happen, we must remove from our backs the heavy incubus of developmentalism. The removal of that incubus needs to begin in kindergarten.

claims.text: Parents of children in the South Bronx prefer Core Knowledge schools over local public schools because they disagree with the public schools' pedagogical theory.
claims.source_passage: It’s because parents have observed the big difference in methods and results and have come to disagree with the pedagogical theory of the public school!

claims.text: Teachers are currently being taught that self-esteem is the most generative principle of human learning and development.
claims.source_passage: our teachers are being taught that the most generative principle of natural human learning and development is self-esteem.

claims.text: Gaining a particular national or tribal culture is not a 'natural' unfolding in the same way physical development is.
claims.source_passage: It’s one thing to describe the universal unfolding of physical development into puberty. It’s quite another to describe as natural the “unfolding” of a child into gaining a particular tribal or national culture.

claims.text: Human mental development is inherently variable and artificial, meaning it is imposed from the outside rather than developed from within.
claims.source_passage: That distinction between physical and cultural “development” makes the path of human mental development inherently variable, and artificial – something imposed from the outside, not developed from within.

claims.text: American parents believe the main aim of kindergarten is learning reading, writing, and arithmetic.
claims.source_passage: American parents take the common-sense view that yes, the main aim of kindergarten is to begin learning reading, writing, and arithmetic

claims.text: Kindergarten teachers view positive self-esteem as the most important goal of kindergarten, while viewing literacy and mathematics as the least important.
claims.source_passage: K-teachers view positive self-esteem as the most important goal and promoting literacy and mathematics skills as the least important.

claims.text: The devotion of American schools of education to the idea of natural development has gravely weakened national literacy and national unity.
claims.source_passage: So powerful is our schools’ devotion to the idea of natural development that it has gravely weakened our national literacy, and with it, our national unity.

claims.text: There is a significant incongruence between the perspectives of kindergarten teachers and parents regarding the primary goals of kindergarten.
claims.source_passage: The results reveal an incongruence between K-teachers’ perspectives and parents.

claims.text: Parents believe advancement in literacy and mathematics skills are the most important goals for kindergarten.
claims.source_passage: Parents believe, however, advancement in literacy and mathematics skills are the most important goal.

claims.text: Literacy and national unity are connected because both depend on shared background knowledge.
claims.source_passage: The two are connected, because literacy depends on shared background knowledge, and so does the emotion of tribal unity.

claims.text: The prioritization of self-esteem as a primary educational goal is inherently linked to a developmental conception of childhood education.
claims.source_passage: Making self-esteem the primary goal is consistent with a developmental conception of childhood education.

claims.text: Teacher training textbooks teach that self-esteem is the driver of motivation and faster development.
claims.source_passage: If you encourage the sense of self-worth, then you will be motivated to be self-confident. You will be motivated generally. You will develop faster and better.

claims.text: The developmental claim of universal naturalness in human growth is contradicted by history and anthropology.
claims.source_passage: But the claim of universal naturalness is problematic – as the disciplines of history and anthropology disclose.

claims.text: Moral development (such as selfishness and empathy) develops differently in different societies based on specific forms of tutelage.
claims.source_passage: For one thing, those conflicting impulses of selfishness and empathy develop differently in different societies under different forms of tutelage.

claims.text: Basic cognitive development, including number sense, varies significantly across different societies.
claims.source_passage: But it’s not just moral development that differs in different societies; even basic development in something as neutral as number sense develops very differently in different societies

claims.text: The child-centered, self-esteem-centered conception of development achieved total dominance in American elementary education by the mid-20th century.
claims.source_passage: Unfortunately, the child-centered, self-esteem-centered conception of development fully took over our elementary education by the mid-20th century

claims.text: The growth of concepts in a child is not an organic, plant-like development but an arduous mental process based on the manipulation of symbols.
claims.source_passage: the “growth” of concepts in the child is not an organic development, but an arduous mental process based upon the active manipulation of symbols by the infant in alliance with sensory inputs; it is not an internal “development” like the growth of a plant from a seed

claims.text: Conceptual growth occurs differently depending on the symbols used in specific languages and cultures.
claims.source_passage: It takes place differently as the symbols are different in different languages and cultures.

claims.text: The change in classroom furniture and arrangement from rows to clusters was caused by a change in educational philosophy.
claims.source_passage: When the education philosophy changed, (which caused our reading scores to decline) so did the furniture change.

claims.text: The educational philosophy responsible for moving to child-centered furniture arrangements also caused American reading scores to decline.
claims.source_passage: When the education philosophy changed, (which caused our reading scores to decline) so did the furniture change.

claims.text: Current child-centered furniture arrangements are physically awkward for teacher-led instruction.
claims.source_passage: Currently, that’s a little awkward. Many children need to turn away from the table to watch the teacher.

claims.text: Romantic developmentalism is the single greatest barrier to educational equality and social justice in modern education.
claims.source_passage: Romantic developmentalism (an educational idea that flourished in Europe in the early nineteenth century and took over American elementary education) is the single greatest barrier to educational equality and social justice in modern education.

claims.text: Developmentalism infantilizes, individualizes, and delays early education rather than integrating children into the social world.
claims.source_passage: It infantilizes and individualizes and delays early education instead of quickly and equally bringing young people into the social world they are to inhabit.

claims.text: Developmentalism prevents schools from overcoming the disadvantages students face at home.
claims.source_passage: It hinders the school from overcoming home disadvantage so that all students can master the public sphere.

claims.text: The rise of developmentalism as a guiding principle in early education is the greatest tragedy of American public schooling in the last century.
claims.source_passage: The gravest tragedy of American public schooling in the past 100 years has been the rise of this “developmentalism” as a chief guiding principle of early education.

claims.text: Developmentalism is a causal factor in sustaining the verbal gap between black and white students.
claims.source_passage: It sustains the black/white verbal gap.

claims.text: The educational philosophy of developmentalism has led to a miseducated adult population, as evidenced by public responses to the COVID pandemic.
claims.source_passage: It has miseducated our adult population – as recent interviews about the COVID pandemic have illustrated.

claims.text: The report 'A Nation at Risk' failed to produce lasting reform because its analysis focused on high schools and ignored early elementary education.
claims.source_passage: The analysis was high-school-centric. It simply assumed that the early grades were doing the basic job. Their recommendations mostly pertained to the high school years.

claims.text: The leadership of the 'A Nation at Risk' study wrongly assumed that early elementary grades were effectively performing their foundational tasks.
claims.source_passage: The college presidents set the tone. The analysis was high-school-centric. It simply assumed that the early grades were doing the basic job.

claims.text: Elementary school teacher candidates spend too much time on education methods courses at the expense of subject matter knowledge.
claims.source_passage: The teacher preparation curriculum is weighted heavily with courses in “educational methods” at the expense of courses in subjects to be taught. A survey of 1,350 institutions training teachers indicated that 41 percent of the time of elementary school teacher candidates is spent in education courses, which reduces the amount of time available for subject matter courses.

claims.text: Adult achievement levels are significantly determined by the amount of background knowledge acquired during early childhood.
claims.source_passage: If only those 1983 university presidents knew then what we know now about the degree to which adult achievement is determined by early background knowledge, their report might have induced a reform of our elementary schools.

claims.text: The takeover of teacher training institutions by developmentalist philosophy in the second half of the 20th century caused the decline in American grade school performance.
claims.source_passage: The chief agency of our grade-school failures since the 1950s has been the developmentalism of the early grades. Developmentalism fully took over our schools and our teacher training institutions in the second half of the twentieth century, when (as the previous SAT chart indicates) the decline began in a big way.

claims.text: Developmentalism fully took over American schools and teacher training institutions in the second half of the twentieth century.
claims.source_passage: Developmentalism fully took over our schools and our teacher training institutions in the second half of the twentieth century

claims.text: The takeover of developmentalism in schools coincides with the beginning of a significant decline in SAT scores.
claims.source_passage: Developmentalism fully took over our schools and our teacher training institutions in the second half of the twentieth century, when (as the previous SAT chart indicates) the decline began in a big way.

claims.text: The Common School philosophy was successful and instrumental in the creation of the United States.
claims.source_passage: But first let’s consider the school philosophy that came before developmentalism–and created the United States.

claims.text: Early Massachusetts schools required both white and free black students to attend the Common School without segregation.
claims.source_passage: Earlier in Massachusetts, where leadership of the American school system resided, there was not even school segregation of black students. All young people, including free black students, were required to attend the Common School.

claims.text: Human beings are less endowed with innate instincts for guidance than animals and therefore require external direction and control.
claims.source_passage: The human being is less endowed with instincts for his guidance than the lower orders of animated creation... All his faculties and powers are susceptible of a right direction and control, and, if obedient to them, blessings innumerable and inexhaustible will be lavished upon him.

claims.text: Without proper social and educational direction, a child's faculties are liable to cause them suffering.
claims.source_passage: But all his powers and faculties are also liable to a wrong direction and control; and, obedient to them, he becomes a living wound, and the universe of encompassing relations presses upon him only to torture him.

claims.text: Republican government requires a higher degree of positive human qualities, such as altruism, than other forms of government.
claims.source_passage: Republican government presupposes the existence of these qualities in a higher degree than any other form.

claims.text: Human nature is defined by a 'double sidedness' where both selfish and altruistic tendencies have evolutionary survival value.
claims.source_passage: Madison’s balanced view is essentially that of present-day evolutionary psychologists, who have explained why humans have evolved the double sidedness that Madison described... both the selfish and altruistic sides of human nature have evolved successfully because both sides have had survival value.

claims.text: The American founders emphasized schooling primarily to inculcate altruism and loyalty to the nation over individual selfishness.
claims.source_passage: Because Madison’s and Mann’s partly skeptical view of human nature prevailed among our founders, they placed a great deal of emphasis on schooling to inculcate altruism and loyalty to the whole tribe.

claims.text: Education should teach students that they belong to the public and the state rather than themselves.
claims.source_passage: Let our pupil be taught that he does not belong to himself, but that he is public property.

claims.text: Elizabeth Peabody was a primary influence in introducing Friedrich Froebel's developmentalist ideas to American education.
claims.source_passage: And one of Emerson’s disciples was Ms. Elizabeth Peabody, who was hugely influential in bringing developmentalism into our schools... She was the introducer of Friedrich Froebel into our intellectual bloodstream.

claims.text: The metaphor of the 'kindergarten' implies that the school is a place for children to grow naturally like plants in a garden.
claims.source_passage: Froebel was the German writer who invented and named the kindergarten. That’s a German word that literally means 'children’s garden.' That is to say: a place where you garden children.

claims.text: Horace Mann and Benjamin Rush were not adherents to the 'religion of nature' that swept Europe after Rousseau in the nineteenth century.
claims.source_passage: Mann and Rush and other American leaders were not adherents to the religion of nature that swept Europe after Rousseau in the nineteenth century.

claims.text: The successors to Horace Mann in Concord, Massachusetts, including Emerson and Thoreau, made the state a 'cradle of the new nature worship' in America.
claims.source_passage: The successors to Horace Mann in Massachusetts – especially in the town of Concord, – the home of Emerson and Thoreau – made the state also a cradle of the new nature worship in America.

claims.text: Elizabeth Peabody was a prime introducer of developmentalism and the ideas of Friedrich Froebel into the American educational system.
claims.source_passage: She was a prime introducer of the developmental idea to our teachers and parents. She was the introducer of Friedrich Froebel into our intellectual bloodstream.

claims.text: Friedrich Froebel chose the metaphor of 'kindergarten' (children's garden) to represent the developmentalist principle of 'gardening' children.
claims.source_passage: Froebel chose that metaphor as ideal for the developmentalist principle. By reading a bit of Froebel one comes to understand why developmentalists in the United States, and especially in our teacher training schools, are so confident of their principles.

claims.text: Modern developmentalists use neutral abstractions like 'constructivism' to mask an underlying religious-like confidence in natural development.
claims.source_passage: They don’t state the underlying religious confidence expressed by Froebel, but the confidence is there. They use neutral abstractions like 'constructivism' and the like. But behind the conviction that 'self-esteem' trumps 'rote learning' lies a firm, Froebelist faith in natural development.

claims.text: Froebel's educational philosophy requires education to be passive and protective rather than active or directive.
claims.source_passage: Education in itself must necessarily be passive, watchfully and protectively following; for the effect of the divine is, when undisturbed, necessarily good: in fact, it cannot be otherwise than good.

claims.text: Forceful instruction or 'dictatorial' education has a destructive effect on the 'divine' and healthy development of the child.
claims.source_passage: All active, dictatorial, invariable, and forcibly interfering education and instruction must necessarily have a disturbing, checking, and destructive effect upon the action of the divine, in accordance with and upon the original, unviolated, and healthy state of the man-being.

claims.text: The central religious faith of developmentalism is the belief in the unspoiled and holy character of the natural.
claims.source_passage: This faith in the child’s inborn instinct is the intense faith in the unspoiled and holy character of the natural. That is the central religious faith of developmentalism.

claims.text: The developmentalist faith in the natural is held as intensely in 2022 as it was in 1890.
claims.source_passage: It is a faith that is held as intensely in 2022 as it was held in 1890.

claims.text: John Dewey’s 'Pedagogic Creed' posits that education is merely 'pressure from without' unless it connects to an activity initiated by the child.
claims.source_passage: Save as the efforts of the educator connect with some activity which the child is carrying on of his own initiative independent of the educator, education becomes reduced to a pressure from without.

claims.text: John Dewey believed that mastery of the social sphere would be instinctive and did not require assertive instruction by the teacher.
claims.source_passage: He does not think any special indoctrination is needed. Mastery of the social will be instinctive. No need for assertive instruction by the teacher.

claims.text: Dewey's theory of developmental social indoctrination fails to acknowledge that a child's instincts might be inherently selfish and in need of correction.
claims.source_passage: That’s a bit more complex than Froebel but by no means acknowledges that the child’s instincts and powers might be inherently selfish and evil, and in need of correction.

claims.text: William Heard Kilpatrick's 1918 article 'The Project Method' was a nation-changing document that recommended a naturalistic, developmental approach to knowledge-gain.
claims.source_passage: William Heard Kilpatrick in the year 1918... issued a nation-changing article, 'The Project Method.' It recommended a naturalistic, developmental approach to early knowledge-gain through working on actual projects.

claims.text: Dewey characterized social indoctrination as a natural developmental process that unfolds on its own.
claims.source_passage: Dewey has made even social indoctrination developmental. It develops on its own, as part of a natural process.

claims.text: Modern educational terms like 'Constructivism,' 'Problem-Based Learning,' and 'Discovery Learning' are simply new names for the 'Project Method.'
claims.source_passage: The project method is now called 'Constructivism,' 'Problem-Based Learning,' 'Discovery Learning,' as well as other names, too.

claims.text: The terminology of developmental education is frequently changed to mask the accurately reported failures of those methods.
claims.source_passage: Many other names, for as soon as somebody accurately reports its failures, it gets an additional name.

claims.text: Mainline researchers find that constructivist and discovery learning methods are invariably ineffective in properly controlled studies.
claims.source_passage: When mainline researchers study its effectiveness in properly controlled studies, the results are invariably negative.

claims.text: Studies supporting developmental methods do not include credible success stories among disadvantaged students.
claims.source_passage: But even the favorable studies reported by adherents do not tend to include credible success stories among disadvantaged students.

claims.text: The triumph of developmental methods in elementary schools caused the 20th-century decline in American reading scores.
claims.source_passage: With the gradual triumph of 'developmental' methods in elementary school, all American reading scores – including those of white students – declined in the twentieth century.

claims.text: Developmentalism is maintained in American education schools with a religious-like conviction rather than scientific evidence.
claims.source_passage: It’s because it is held with religious conviction by those disciples of Dewey and Peabody and Kilpatrick who still instruct our teachers.

claims.text: The neocortex is biologically a blank page (tabula rasa) that is neither instinctual nor pre-programmed.
claims.source_passage: The latest evolved part of the brain – the neocortex–is basically a blank page ready to be written upon... That neocortex, where schooling mainly takes place, is neither instinctual nor pre-programed.

claims.text: A young child’s actual instinct is to wait for instructions which they then assimilate and correlate.
claims.source_passage: The young child’s actual instinct is to wait for instructions – which it then eagerly assimilates and actively correlates.

claims.text: Teacher training textbooks intentionally attempt to preserve Piagetian theory despite recognizing its scientific deficiencies.
claims.source_passage: The authors have it both ways in this 2019 edition, one way for the knowledgeable scientist who has repudiated the stage theory of Piaget, and another to please the education professor who is devoted to developmentalism.

claims.text: A child’s growing understanding of the world is profoundly influenced by interactions with family, peers, and teachers within a cultural backdrop rather than being purely biological.
claims.source_passage: In reality, a child’s effort to understand her world is far more social than Piaget described. Her growing understanding of the world is profoundly influenced by interactions with family members, peers, and teachers, and it takes place against the backdrop of cultural values.

claims.text: Contemporary teacher textbooks contain logical contradictions, simultaneously claiming Piaget's stage theory is invalid while asserting it should not be abandoned.
claims.source_passage: My cavil is this-The authors have it both ways in this 2019 edition, one way for the knowledgeable scientist who has repudiated the stage theory of Piaget, and another to please the education professor who is devoted to developmentalism and some version of universal stage theory.

claims.text: The theory of universal human 'unfolding' is invalid because research shows human development is dependent on specific cultural values.
claims.source_passage: You can’t logically say that “unfolding” is universal and then say that the “unfolding” is not universal but is dependent on culture. Research has determined that only the second is correct.

claims.text: Human development is fundamentally bound by language and specific knowledge rather than universal biological stages.
claims.source_passage: Human development is language bound and knowledge bound.

claims.text: Schools are not facilitating a natural unfolding of the child but are instead instructing children in a specific national ethnicity.
claims.source_passage: The schools of different nations are not assisting in a universal unfolding. Rather, they are instructing children in an ethnicity – how to become mature, contributing members of a particular nation

claims.text: The dominance of developmentalism in the United States has caused a decline in national reading ability and language skill.
claims.source_passage: Because of the triumph of developmentalism in the United States, reading ability and language skill in the United States in recent decades have gone downhill

claims.text: Educational unfairness and the achievement gap are caused by the American faith in natural development over direct instruction.
claims.source_passage: Our reading decline and our persistent unfairness between groups are both owing to our current faith in the superiority of natural development over artificial instruction.

claims.text: There is no innate biological blueprint for literacy in the human brain.
claims.source_passage: But there is no internal blueprint for literacy – which arrived on the evolutionary scene far too recently for current nature to take notice.

claims.text: Active instruction from the outside is the only effective method for achieving literacy.
claims.source_passage: Active instruction of the child from the outside is the only literacy blueprint available.

claims.text: Literacy instruction must explicitly include social traditions and moral imperatives.
claims.source_passage: Literacy instruction must include not only the language of the tribe, but also the traditions, and moral imperatives of the tribe. These do not unfold. They must be explicitly taught.

claims.text: Developmentalism is a modern, secular version of the Pelagian heresy which posits that human nature is inherently good and reliable if allowed to develop naturally.
claims.source_passage: theologically speaking, developmentalism is an updated version of the Pelagian heresy that human nature if allowed to develop naturally is good and can be relied on.

claims.text: Nature is not a reliable guide for determining educational readiness or curriculum for reading and mathematics.
claims.source_passage: Nature is not a reliable guide to anything much in human schooling – not to “readiness,” not to reading, not to math.

claims.text: Mathematics instruction is dependent on a child's ethnicity and cultural background rather than just natural development.
claims.source_passage: Even math instruction turns out to depend on the child’s ethnicity, as Professor Stevenson and his colleagues have shown.

claims.text: Human survival and dominance are the result of artificial, non-instinctual activities rather than accord with nature.
claims.source_passage: Our artificiality, not our accord with nature, has done the trick.

claims.text: Naturalistic and project-based learning models are elitist and do not function with equal effect for rich and poor children.
claims.source_passage: Naturalistic, project-based learning is, moreover, elitist. It does not work efficiently, nor with equal effect upon rich and poor.

claims.text: Project-based and constructivist learning methods actively undermine educational fairness.
claims.source_passage: Project-based, constructivist learning is the enemy of fairness.

claims.text: The persistence of developmentalism in schools is driven by the ideological belief in education schools that the 'natural' is inherently good and the 'artificial' is bad.
claims.source_passage: “It’s mainly because our education schools teach, and our schools continue to believe, that the natural is good and the artificial is bad.”

claims.text: The human child lacks reliable instinctive guidance, necessitating social and artificial instruction.
claims.source_passage: as Horace Mann correctly observed, nature has withheld reliable instinctive guidance from the human child.

claims.text: Developmentalism is logically inconsistent because it advocates for a 'natural' pace while blaming 'unnatural' home disadvantages for the slow progress of poor children.
claims.source_passage: Advocates of developmentalism do not credibly explain why, if children should proceed at their nature-determined pace, do rich children proceed faster than poor children. ... Note the inconsistency in that view!

claims.text: Effective, non-developmental schooling in early grades can gradually overcome the handicaps associated with home disadvantage.
claims.source_passage: non-developmental, non-project-based schooling, starting in kindergarten, does gradually overcome home disadvantage in effective schools in the early grades.

claims.text: Early human education is not a process of unfolding in stages, and school progress depends more on prior knowledge than on biological age.
claims.source_passage: it is demonstrable that early human education does not consist of an unfolding in stages, and that children’s progress in the school is less dependent on the child’s age than on what the child has already learned.

claims.text: Discovery centers are a suboptimal pedagogical tool compared to whole-class learning.
claims.source_passage: Discovery centers are a hallmark of constructivist developmentalism. Discovery centers are suboptimal compared to whole-class learning.

claims.text: Language proficiency, rather than chronological age, is the primary determinant of educational attainment.
claims.source_passage: language proficiency, not age, determines educational attainment.

claims.text: Requiring children to 'construct' their own knowledge is a waste of time that penalizes all students.
claims.source_passage: To focus early education on “the construction of knowledge” by the child’s own efforts is a profligate waste of time that penalizes any child, rich or poor.

claims.text: Discovery centers are a hallmark of constructivist developmentalism.
claims.source_passage: Discovery centers are a hallmark of constructivist developmentalism.

claims.text: Focusing early education on the child's 'construction of knowledge' through their own efforts is a profligate waste of time.
claims.source_passage: To focus early education on “the construction of knowledge” by the child’s own efforts is a profligate waste of time that penalizes any child, rich or poor.

claims.text: Developmentalism punishes poor children more severely than rich children.
claims.source_passage: But it punishes the poor much more than the rich because poor children are getting ineffective instruction in both the home and the school, while rich children are gaining lots of knowledge at home.

claims.text: The slowness and inefficiency inherent in developmentalism penalizes any nation that adopts the philosophy.
claims.source_passage: The slowness and inefficiency of developmentalism penalizes any nation that adopts it.

claims.text: Developmentalism is the primary cause of the United States' low standing (25th) in international PISA rankings.
claims.source_passage: It’s the chief case of our poor showing (twenty-fifth among the nations on the international PISA rankings).

claims.text: The black/white language gap can be overcome through changes in educational approach.
claims.source_passage: It is a gap we shall overcome.

claims.text: The cultural preference for organic foods is a modern expression of the providential religious faith in nature's rightness.
claims.source_passage: We nod to nature’s providential character every time we choose “organic” foods, and every time we choose a natural approach to early childhood education.

claims.text: The 'two souls' concept in Goethe's Faust and human doubleness in Shakespeare serve as historical/literary evidence for the anti-Pelagian view of human nature.
claims.source_passage: Shakespeare was also an anti-Pelagian who emphasized our innate mixture of good and evil. In one of his sonnets (144) he confronts those two angels within himself. And there’s a memorable moment in Goethe’s Faust, when Faust complains that “two souls dwell in my breast.”

claims.text: Selfishness is more successful than altruism within a single group, but altruistic groups outperform selfish groups in broader competition.
claims.source_passage: In a famous article, David S. Wilson and E. O. Wilson summed it up this way: “Selfishness beats altruism within groups. Altruistic groups beat selfish groups. Everything else is commentary.”

claims.text: The sociocultural force listed in developmental textbooks is qualitatively different and more 'unnatural' than biological or psychological forces of development.
claims.source_passage: According to that view, human development is being generated by four forces: biological, psychological, sociocultural, and life-cycle forces. But note how unnatural is one of the listed forces – the “socio-cultural.”

claims.text: Self-esteem peaks in preschool and typically declines in early elementary school as children begin comparing themselves to peers.
claims.source_passage: Self-esteem is normally very high in preschool children but declines gradually during the early elementary school years as children compare themselves to others.

claims.text: A Nation at Risk was biased toward high schools because the study group was led by university presidents who assumed elementary schools were already succeeding.
claims.source_passage: The study group for A Nation at Risk was controlled by estimable people who were presidents of top universities... The analysis was high-school-centric. It simply assumed that the early grades were doing the basic job.

claims.text: Through unconscious education, an individual naturally inherits the 'funded capital of civilization.'
claims.source_passage: Through this unconscious education the individual gradually comes to share in the intellectual and moral resources which humanity has succeeded in getting together. He becomes an inheritor of the funded capital of civilization.

claims.text: Evolution favored the development of the neocortex as a blank page to allow for adaptive flexibility rather than instinctual programming.
claims.source_passage: Nature, through evolution, has determined that inventing how to accommodate to current circumstances is more helpful to survival than being born with a developmental blueprint. The latest evolved part of the brain – the neocortex–is basically a blank page ready to be written upon.

claims.text: The human species' ability to fly and its independence from climate are proofs of the superiority of artificiality over natural instinct.
claims.source_passage: What other large species is so independent of climate and is so widely distributed? Or can fly such great distances unnaturally through the air? Our artificiality, not our accord with nature, has done the trick.

claims.text: Mainstream teacher textbooks maintain that Piaget's theory should not be abandoned despite admitting that research shows culture overrides his 'universal' stages.
claims.source_passage: After explaining why Piaget’s theory is invalid because culture overrides nature... they write: “These criticisms do not mean that Piaget’s theory is invalid or should be abandoned.”

evidence.description: A meta-theoretical statement on sociobiology asserting that while selfishness wins within groups, altruistic groups outperform selfish ones.
evidence.source_reference: David Sloan Wilson and E. O. Wilson: “Rethinking the Theoretical Foundations of Sociobiology,” 2007
evidence.source_passage: Selfishness beats altruism within groups. Altruistic groups beat selfish groups. Everything else is commentary.

evidence.description: Historical contrast between early American schoolbooks (which emphasized guidance) and modern child-centered texts.
evidence.source_reference: Not specified; general reference to earlier American schoolbooks
evidence.source_passage: Our earlier view (just check those earlier American schoolbooks) had been that the child’s instincts and powers are a mixed affair which need to be given content and guidance.

evidence.description: Data showing the decline of social cohesion and the rise of hyper-individualism in the United States over the last century.
evidence.source_reference: Robert D. Putnam, The Upswing, 2020
evidence.source_passage: Robert Putnam observes in his new book Upswing, which shows that hyper-individualism (the “I” over the “WE”) has recently dominated in America and has weakened our cohesion.

evidence.description: Literary and theological examples of the 'two souls' or internal duality of man (Shakespeare, Goethe, Augustine).
evidence.source_reference: Shakespeare (Sonnet 144), Goethe (Faust), St. Augustine
evidence.source_passage: Our deepest and most accurate writers have been anti-Pelagians like Augustine and Shakespeare, and Goethe.

evidence.description: Robert Putnam's research showing the rise of hyper-individualism and its negative impact on social cohesion.
evidence.source_reference: Robert D. Putnam, The Upswing (2020)
evidence.source_passage: hyper-individualism (the “I” over the “WE”) has recently dominated in America and has weakened our cohesion.

evidence.description: The high demand for Core Knowledge (CK) schools in the South Bronx, where 25,000 children apply for 135 spots.
evidence.source_reference: Hirsch, observations in the South Bronx
evidence.source_passage: parents of some 25,000 children in the South Bronx try every year to place their child in one of the seven Bronx Core Knowledge (CK) schools

evidence.description: A study showing a direct conflict between what parents and kindergarten teachers believe are the primary goals of schooling.
evidence.source_reference: Sverdlov, A., & Aram, D. (2016)
evidence.source_passage: K-teachers view positive self-esteem as the most important goal and promoting literacy and mathematics skills as the least important. Parents believe, however, advancement in literacy and mathematics skills are the most important goal.

evidence.description: Recent findings in developmental psychology and neuroscience showing mental development is artificial and external.
evidence.source_reference: Susan Carey, Nir Kalisman, and Harold Stevenson
evidence.source_passage: This is exactly the conclusion being reached by the latest thinking in developmental psychology – especially in the work of the three scientists... Susan Carey, Nir Kalisman, and Harold Stevenson.

evidence.description: Analysis of a standard teacher training textbook illustrating the emphasis on self-esteem and 'natural' sociocultural forces.
evidence.source_reference: Kail, Robert V.; Cavanaugh, John C. (2019)
evidence.source_passage: I base my claim that developmental determinism and the primacy of self- esteem are still being taught by analyzing a still-widely used teacher textbook revised in 2019

evidence.description: Analysis of a representative teacher training textbook showing 97 references to self-esteem versus 50 for altruism and zero for patriotism.
evidence.source_reference: Kail, Robert V.; Cavanaugh, John C. Human Development: A Life-Span View (2019).
evidence.source_passage: To be fair to the authors, while there are ninety-seven references to self-esteem, there are also fifty references to altruism, though none to patriotism.

evidence.description: Research showing that basic numerical development differs significantly between American, Chinese, and Japanese children.
evidence.source_reference: Stevenson, Harold W., et al. (1990). Contexts of Achievement.
evidence.source_passage: basic development in something as neutral as number sense develops very differently in different societies, as Harold Stevenson and his associates have shown.

evidence.description: Historical analysis of the shift toward individualism in American society during the mid-20th century.
evidence.source_reference: Robert D. Putnam, The Upswing (2020).
evidence.source_passage: the child-centered, self-esteem-centered conception of development fully took over our elementary education by the mid-20th century, along with individualism generally, as Robert Putnam has shown.

evidence.description: Cognitive science research demonstrating that the acquisition of concepts is a constructive symbol-based effort rather than natural maturation.
evidence.source_reference: Susan Carey, The Origin of Concepts (2009).
evidence.source_passage: Susan Carey has shown that the “growth” of concepts in the child is not an organic development, but an arduous mental process based upon the active manipulation of symbols

evidence.description: The report 'A Nation at Risk' (1983) documented the decline of U.S. educational performance relative to other nations.
evidence.source_reference: U.S. Government, A Nation at Risk, 1983

evidence.description: A chart showing the decline of SAT Verbal scores by decade from 1952 to 2012, showing a sharp descent after the mid-century.
evidence.source_reference: SAT Verbal scores 1952–2012 chart

evidence.description: A survey of 1,350 teacher training institutions showing that 41% of elementary teacher training is spent on 'education courses' rather than subject matter.
evidence.source_reference: A Nation at Risk, 1983 survey

evidence.description: A 1940 'March of Time' program featuring William Heard Kilpatrick and John Dewey demonstrating the early enthusiasm for developmentalism.
evidence.source_reference: March of Time, 1940

evidence.description: The publication of the Common School Journal beginning in 1838 as a vehicle for Horace Mann's educational philosophy.
James Madison's Federalist 55, describing the mixture of depravity and estimable qualities in human nature.
Socio-biological principle stated by David Sloan Wilson and E. O. Wilson: 'Selfishness beats altruism within groups. Altruistic groups beat selfish groups.'
evidence.source_reference: Wilson and Wilson, 'Rethinking the Theoretical Foundations of Sociobiology' (2007)

evidence.description: Benjamin Rush's 1786 proposal for public education in Pennsylvania, arguing students should be viewed as 'public property.'
evidence.source_reference: Rush, 'Thoughts Upon the Mode of Education Proper in a Republic' (1786)

evidence.description: Elizabeth Peabody's 1890 translation of Friedrich Froebel which introduced his ideas to the American public.
The etymology and literal meaning of the German word 'kindergarten' as 'children's garden.'
John Dewey's 1897 'Pedagogic Creed' which formalizes child-centered education.
William Heard Kilpatrick's 1918 article 'The Project Method' as a pivotal text for naturalistic learning.
An international study by psychologists analyzing why minimal guidance in instruction fails across multiple methods.
evidence.source_reference: Kirschner, Sweller, and Clark (2006), 'Why Minimal Guidance During Instruction Does Not Work'
evidence.source_passage: A decisive summary was recently provided in a study by an international team of psychologists in an article entitled: 'Why Minimal Guidance During Instruction Does Not Work: An Analysis of the Failure of Constructivist, Discovery, Problem-Based, Experiential and Inquiry-Based Teaching.'

evidence.description: Historical report on the decline of American literacy and educational standards.
evidence.source_reference: A Nation at Risk (1983)
evidence.source_passage: That disaster was reported in A Nation at Risk.

evidence.description: Cognitive science research characterizing the neocortical microcircuit as a tabula rasa.
evidence.source_reference: Kalisman, Silberberg, Markram and Merzenich (2005), 'The Neocortical Microcircuit as a Tabula Rasa'
evidence.source_passage: The latest evolved part of the brain – the neocortex–is basically a blank page ready to be written upon, as the cognitive scientist, Nir Kalisman stated in a memorable article entitled 'The Neocortical Microcircuit as a Tabula Rasa.'

evidence.description: A standard teacher-training textbook used to demonstrate the continued promotion of developmental models.
evidence.source_reference: Kail and Kavanaugh, 'Human Development: A Life-Span View' (2019)
evidence.source_passage: This is its most recent final comment on Piaget, the most popular developmental stage theorist in our education schools... These criticisms do not mean that Piaget’s theory is invalid or should be abandoned.

evidence.description: Advantaged Chinese children learn to count to 20 months earlier than English learners due to language characteristics, while American children learn 'one' and 'two' earlier for similar reasons.
evidence.source_reference: Stevenson, Harold W., et al. (1990)
evidence.source_passage: According to pathbreaking work by Harold Stevenson and his colleagues, advantaged Chinese children learn to count to 20 many months earlier than do advantaged English learners. That is because of certain characteristics of the two languages.

evidence.description: International assessment data showing the continued downward trend of American student performance.
evidence.source_reference: PISA reports

evidence.description: An analysis of a widely used 2019 teacher textbook that maintains developmentalist theories despite scientific repudiation.
evidence.source_reference: Kail, Robert V.; Cavanaugh, John C. Human Development: A Life-Span View (2019)
evidence.source_passage: I base my claim that developmental determinism and the primacy of self- esteem are still being taught by analyzing a still-widely used teacher textbook revised in 2019

evidence.description: Literary analysis of Shakespeare's King Lear showing the character Edmund as a personification of the dangers of following 'Nature' over social law.
evidence.source_reference: Shakespeare, King Lear
evidence.source_passage: Thou, Nature, art my goddess; to thy law My services are bound... Thus did Shakespeare accuse nature.

evidence.description: Professor John McWhorter's argument that spelling and phonics require direct, non-natural instruction.
evidence.source_reference: John McWhorter, The New York Times, 2021
evidence.source_passage: recently felt he needed to write a piece explaining yet again why spelling and phonics need to be taught directly and not encouraged to unfold “naturally” and “developmentally.”

evidence.description: Harold Stevenson's study showing math achievement varies by cultural/ethnic context (American vs. Asian children).
evidence.source_reference: Stevenson, Harold W., et al. (1990)
evidence.source_passage: Even math instruction turns out to depend on the child’s ethnicity, as Professor Stevenson and his colleagues have shown.

evidence.description: Current research in evolutionary psychology and brain studies contradicting the 'natural' slow pace of developmentalism.
evidence.source_reference: Kalisman et al. (2005); Wilson & Wilson (2007)
evidence.source_passage: inconsistent not just with the ancient philosophers – but as we now understand, with nature herself, according to current evolutionary psychology, sociobiology, and current brain studies.

evidence.description: The decline in international educational rankings cited as proof of the failure of project-based learning.
evidence.source_reference: Not explicitly cited in this section, but referred to as documented failure
evidence.source_passage: its failure is documented in the steady decline in our international educational ranking

evidence.description: Data showing disadvantaged children in non-developmental schools outperforming advantaged children in child-centered schools by grade six.
evidence.source_reference: Hirsch (referencing Chapter 1 data)
evidence.source_passage: In fact, by grade six, disadvantaged children are ahead of their advantaged age group who have undergone a child-centered developmental education down the street.

evidence.description: Susan Carey's research demonstrating that attainment is tied to language, not age.
evidence.source_reference: Carey, S. The Origin of Concepts (2009)
evidence.source_passage: Professor Susan Carey, whose brilliant research has established that language proficiency, not age, determines educational attainment.

evidence.description: Research establishing that language proficiency, not age, determines educational success.
evidence.source_reference: Susan Carey, The Origin of Concepts (2009)

evidence.description: International PISA rankings showing the United States ranked 25th among nations.
evidence.source_reference: PISA rankings (implied)

evidence.description: Critique of discovery centers and evidence they are suboptimal compared to whole-class instruction.
evidence.source_reference: Neuman, Susan B.; Wright, Tanya S. All About Words
evidence.source_passage: Discovery centers are suboptimal compared to whole-class learning.

concepts.author_definition: An educational doctrine based on the individualistic assumption that a child's natural instincts and 'natural development' are providential and should guide their education.
concepts.common_alternative: A neutral study of child development milestones.
concepts.source_passage: The newer, developmental view, which became dominant in America by mid-twentieth century, assumes that the child’s natural instincts are providential. When they are followed with empathy, the child will flourish.

concepts.author_definition: An individualistic approach to schooling where the child's inborn nature and interests dictate the curriculum, rather than adult-selected communal content.
The view (adopted from Augustine) that humans are born with an innate mixture of good and evil/selfishness and altruism, requiring social and educational formation.
A functional term for the 'communicative store' of shared background knowledge required for literacy and tribal unity.
A 'non-existent' concept in literacy education where children are assumed to gain reading proficiency through diverse, uncoordinated materials rather than specific shared knowledge.
In the context of teacher training, it is erroneously viewed as the most generative principle of human development, often prioritized over literacy and math.
The educational belief that children's minds develop naturally and spontaneously like plants growing from seeds, requiring only a supportive environment rather than direct instruction.
concepts.source_passage: The natural-growth metaphor is still powerfully dominant among our teachers who have been trained in many of our schools of education.

concepts.author_definition: A focus in teacher training that emphasizes the individual child's ego, self-worth, and internal moral stages over collective identity or patriotism.
concepts.source_passage: This is development as self-centered individualism. To be fair to the authors, while there are ninety-seven references to self-esteem, there are also fifty references to altruism, though none to patriotism.

concepts.author_definition: An inclusive, non-segregated educational system designed to provide shared knowledge and moral instruction to create a unified republican citizenry.
The evolutionary reality that humans possess both selfish instincts and altruistic capacities, the latter of which must be prioritized through education.
The naturalist view of education as 'gardening' children—allowing them to develop according to their own nature rather than through social direction.
concepts.source_passage: Froebel was the German writer who invented and named the kindergarten. That’s a German word that literally means 'children’s garden.' That is to say: a place where you garden children.

concepts.author_definition: A 'children's garden' where the role of the educator is to 'garden' children, allowing them to unfold according to natural laws rather than social direction.
concepts.common_alternative: A pre-school educational institution for young children.
concepts.source_passage: That’s a German word that literally means 'children’s garden.' That is to say: a place where you garden children. Froebel chose that metaphor as ideal for the developmentalist principle.

concepts.author_definition: A 'neutral abstraction' used by modern educators to describe a pedagogical approach that is actually rooted in Froebelian natural religion.
concepts.source_passage: They use neutral abstractions like 'constructivism' and the like. But behind the conviction that 'self-esteem' trumps 'rote learning' lies a firm, Froebelist faith in natural development.

concepts.author_definition: An educational approach that is 'watchfully and protectively following' the child's own development rather than directing it.
concepts.source_passage: Education in itself must necessarily be passive, watchfully and protectively following; for the effect of the divine is, when undisturbed, necessarily good

concepts.author_definition: A naturalistic, developmental approach to early knowledge acquisition through student-led projects, which Hirsch identifies as the precursor to Constructivism.
The cognitive science concept that the neocortex is born as a blank slate without instinctual academic content, making it highly dependent on external instruction and social input.
The developmentalist belief that children grow and learn according to internal biological laws that act in each individual independently of culture.
concepts.source_passage: We know now that the child does not “beautifully unfold, and grow well, in conformity with the laws which act in each individual.”

concepts.author_definition: A society's customs, laws, and moral restraints that hold it together and constrain individual impulse.
concepts.source_passage: “The curiosity of nations” – that means a society’s customs, its laws and moral restraints that hold a society together.

concepts.author_definition: The theological belief that human nature is naturally good and can be relied upon to develop properly without external intervention.
concepts.source_passage: developmentalism is an updated version of the Pelagian heresy that human nature if allowed to develop naturally is good and can be relied on.

concepts.author_definition: The non-instinctual, culturally-constructed activities and knowledge (like flying or formal math) that characterize human survival and distinctiveness.
A hallmark of constructivist pedagogy where children explore topics independently; Hirsch identifies these as suboptimal and a waste of instructional time.
A hallmark of constructivist developmentalism where children explore and discover new information about topics (like plants) through their own efforts rather than explicit instruction.
The educational theory that children should build their own understanding of the world through self-directed exploration rather than being taught systematically.
A burdensome, outdated educational philosophy that hinders reform and national achievement.
The collective intellectual and moral resources accumulated by humanity, which Dewey believed children inherit unconsciously.
A Shakespearean term used to describe the customs, laws, and moral restraints of a society that constrain individual impulse.
A metaphor for the child in Piagetian theory, describing an individual trying to coordinate theory with data in isolation from social context.
Instructional methods including constructivist, discovery, and problem-based learning that have been identified as failures by cognitive scientists.

cases.name: Mid-twentieth century American elementary education
cases.description: The period during which Hirsch claims developmentalism and child-centered individualism completely supplanted the earlier model of content-based, adult-guided instruction.
key_passages: individualistic doctrines of developmentalism which completely took over our elementary education by mid-twentieth century.

cases.name: South Bronx Core Knowledge Schools
cases.description: A natural experiment in parental choice where 25,000 families compete for 135 kindergarten spots in schools using the Core Knowledge curriculum, demonstrating a popular rejection of standard 'developmentalist' public school methods.
key_passages: parents of some 25,000 children in the South Bronx try every year to place their child in one of the seven Bronx Core Knowledge (CK) schools where there are just 135 kindergarten places.

They have come to think that the kindergartens of the public school are a waste of valuable time – not because those schools lack resources... It’s because parents have observed the big difference in methods and results

cases.name: The US Elementary Classroom Furniture Shift
cases.description: A physical transformation of American classrooms from rows of wooden desks with storage to clustered tables and 'flexible' seating. Hirsch argues this transition was the physical manifestation of a shift in educational philosophy from teacher-led to child-centered instruction.
key_passages: In grade schools across the nation, we have changed the composition and arrangement of the classroom furniture. That physical change did not make our reading scores decline. But the reasons for the furniture change did make the scores decline.
One good thing about those old wooden desks. They had spaces under the top, where you could store your pen and pencil and paper and a book or two.
That’s the look of the classroom under 'child-centered' education.
contestable_aspects: The causal link between classroom arrangement/philosophy and declining reading scores.
The characterization of furniture as an indicator of academic decline.

cases.name: A Nation at Risk (1983)
cases.description: A federal study that warned of the decline in American education but failed to solve the problem because it focused on high schools and ignored the developmentalist takeover of early elementary education.
key_passages: A Nation at Risk... showed that things had become worse educationally for the United States in comparison to other nations.
The analysis was high-school-centric. It simply assumed that the early grades were doing the basic job.

cases.name: The Massachusetts Common School
cases.description: Under Horace Mann's leadership in the 1830s, this system became the model for American schooling, emphasizing mandatory attendance for all students, regardless of race, and focusing on moral and social direction.
key_passages: In Massachusetts, where leadership of the American school system resided, there was not even school segregation of black students. All young people, including free black students, were required to attend the Common School.

cases.name: Concord Nature Worship
cases.description: The intellectual environment of Concord, Massachusetts, home to Emerson and Thoreau, which Hirsch identifies as the American cradle for the 'nature religion' that led to developmentalism.
key_passages: especially in the town of Concord, – the home of Emerson and Thoreau – made the state also a cradle of the new nature worship in America.

cases.name: Concord, Massachusetts
cases.description: Described as the 'cradle of the new nature worship' in America and home to transcendentalist thinkers Emerson and Thoreau who influenced the spread of developmentalist ideas.
key_passages: The successors to Horace Mann in Massachusetts – especially in the town of Concord, – the home of Emerson and Thoreau – made the state also a cradle of the new nature worship in America.

cases.name: Chinese vs. American child development comparison
cases.description: A study comparing when children learn specific mathematical concepts (counting to 20 vs. understanding 'one' and 'two'). It found that Chinese children count earlier due to language structure, while American children understand small numbers earlier for similar linguistic reasons, proving development is language-bound rather than a universal biological unfolding.
key_passages: advantaged Chinese children learn to count to 20 many months earlier than do advantaged English learners. That is because of certain characteristics of the two languages. However, American children learn the meaning of “one” and “two” six months earlier than Chinese children do.
contestable_aspects: The degree to which linguistic differences account for the entirety of the developmental gap vs. other cultural factors.

cases.name: The Grade Six Reversal
cases.description: A comparison where disadvantaged children attending structured, non-developmental schools eventually surpass advantaged peers who were educated using child-centered, developmental methods.
key_passages: In fact, by grade six, disadvantaged children are ahead of their advantaged age group who have undergone a child-centered developmental education down the street.

thinkers.name: John Dewey
thinkers.key_idea: The principle that the child’s “instincts and powers” should guide her education.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch identifies Dewey as the source of the individualistic principle governing child-centered education, which he argues is based on a flawed assumption of natural providence.
key_passages: on the principle, stated by John Dewey, that the child’s “instincts and powers” should guide her education.

thinkers.name: St. Augustine
thinkers.key_idea: The belief that humans are born with 'guilt' or an 'infected mixture' of good and bad rather than being naturally innocent.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch aligns with Augustine's 'anti-Pelagian' view of human doubleness, using it as a precursor to modern sociobiology's view of selfish vs. altruistic impulses.
key_passages: Augustine (a good observer of the dualism in humans) said: “Yes! We are still guilty of Adam’s disobedience and are still an infected mixture of good and bad.”

thinkers.name: Robert Putnam
thinkers.key_idea: The historical shift from a 'WE' oriented culture to hyper-individualism ('I').
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Putnam's data to argue that the 'I' over 'WE' focus has weakened American cohesion and that school-based developmentalism has promoted this shift.
key_passages: Robert Putnam observes in his new book Upswing, which shows that hyper-individualism (the “I” over the “WE”) has recently dominated in America and has weakened our cohesion.

thinkers.name: Susan Carey
thinkers.key_idea: The origin of concepts and the artificiality of mental development.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch cites Carey to support the theoretical claim that mental development is not a natural unfolding but is imposed from the outside.
key_passages: This is exactly the conclusion being reached by the latest thinking in developmental psychology – especially in the work of the three scientists... Susan Carey

thinkers.name: Nir Kalisman
thinkers.key_idea: The neocortical microcircuit as a tabula rasa.
thinkers.engagement: Cited to provide a neurological/biological basis for the claim that culture and knowledge are not 'natural' but must be taught.
key_passages: Nir Kalisman... The Neocortical Microcircuit as a tabula rasa

thinkers.name: Lawrence Kohlberg
thinkers.key_idea: Universal, eight stages of moral unfolding that occur naturally in all children.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch argues that Kohlberg's theory of universal natural development is problematic because moral development (selfishness vs. empathy) is culturally dependent and varies across societies.
key_passages: The chief moral idea is that of Kohlberg’s universal, eight stages of moral unfolding. According to the developmental theory, mental growth is natural to each child’s temperament and is promoted by self-esteem. But the claim of universal naturalness is problematic

thinkers.name: William Heard Kilpatrick
thinkers.key_idea: A key player in promoting developmentalism and child-centered education in the early 20th century.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch identifies him as a primary figure responsible for the shift toward developmentalism in American schools, citing his appearance in the 'March of Time' program.
key_passages: Kilpatrick and Dewey were key players

thinkers.name: Elizabeth Peabody
thinkers.key_idea: The person who introduced the developmental ideas of Friedrich Froebel to the United States.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch suggests she may have been even more influential than Dewey and Kilpatrick in the early implementation of developmental ideas.
key_passages: another early player who may have been even more influential – Elizabeth Peabody, who introduced the developmental ideas of Friedrich Froebel.

thinkers.name: Horace Mann
thinkers.key_idea: Human faculties are 'darkness' without social control; education must provide the 'right direction' that instincts lack.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch quotes Mann extensively to show that the leader of the American school system consciously rejected Rousseau's naturalism.
key_passages: All his faculties and powers are susceptible of a right direction and control, and, if obedient to them, blessings innumerable and inexhaustible will be lavished upon him.

thinkers.name: James Madison
thinkers.key_idea: Human nature is a mix of depravity and qualities that justify confidence; republicanism depends on the latter.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch aligns Madison's political philosophy with modern evolutionary psychology to justify socialized education.
key_passages: As there is a degree of depravity in mankind which requires a certain degree of circumspection and distrust, so there are other qualities in human nature which justify a certain portion of esteem and confidence.

thinkers.name: Benjamin Rush
thinkers.key_idea: The student belongs to the state ('public property') and must be taught to prioritize the welfare of the country over family.
thinkers.engagement: Used to illustrate the intense moral and social 'solicitude' of the early American educational vision.
key_passages: Let our pupil be taught that he does not belong to himself, but that he is public property.

thinkers.name: Friedrich Froebel
thinkers.key_idea: Education should be a passive process of 'gardening' children, based on the belief that the natural/divine state of a child is inherently good.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch identifies Froebel as the source of the 'religious confidence' that underlies modern developmentalism, arguing that his metaphors have led to a rejection of necessary directive instruction.
key_passages: He was the introducer of Friedrich Froebel into our intellectual bloodstream.
Education in itself must necessarily be passive, watchfully and protectively following; for the effect of the divine is, when undisturbed, necessarily good

thinkers.name: Jean Piaget
thinkers.key_idea: Developmental stage theory where the child acts as a 'lone scientist.'
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch critiques Piaget for undervaluing social and cultural environments and notes that current research has repudiated his stage theory.
key_passages: Piaget describes the child as a lone scientist, constantly trying to figure out by herself how her theory coordinates with data.

thinkers.name: Harold Stevenson
thinkers.key_idea: Cross-cultural studies of child achievement showing that math skills are language-dependent.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Stevenson's data to refute the idea of universal biological unfolding and to support the claim that development is knowledge and language bound.
key_passages: According to pathbreaking work by Harold Stevenson and his colleagues, advantaged Chinese children learn to count to 20 many months earlier than do advantaged English learners.

thinkers.name: Paula Lombardi
thinkers.key_idea: Direct instruction is often unfairly maligned by educators as outdated or pedantic.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch quotes Lombardi to explain why necessary pedagogical methods (direct instruction) are being rejected in American schools despite their necessity for literacy.
key_passages: In recent decades, the concept of direct instruction has taken on negative associations among some educators.

thinkers.name: John McWhorter
thinkers.key_idea: Spelling and phonics must be taught directly and do not unfold naturally.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch cites McWhorter as a modern 'anti-Pelagian' literacy expert who defends the necessity of direct instruction over developmental 'unfolding.'
key_passages: Professor John McWhorter of Columbia University... recently felt he needed to write a piece explaining yet again why spelling and phonics need to be taught directly

thinkers.name: Susan Neuman
thinkers.key_idea: Strong advocate for explicit vocabulary instruction, though still utilizes some 'Discovery Center' methods.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch praises her support for systematic literacy but critiques her lingering use of constructivist 'discovery' methods.
key_passages: all honor to Professor Neuman for her strong support of explicit and systematic literacy instruction
even Professor Neuman advocates some of the developmental, indirect, naturalistic pedagogy... For instance, in All About Words, she says: 'We created a 'Discovery Center''

actors.name: John Dewey
actors.context: Philosophical architect of child-centered individualism.

actors.name: Robert Putnam
actors.context: Author of The Upswing, providing data on the rise of individualism.

actors.name: St. Augustine
actors.context: Theologian used to illustrate the duality of human nature.

actors.name: South Bronx Parents
actors.context: Represent 'common sense' by valuing literacy/math and seeking out Core Knowledge schools.

actors.name: Kindergarten Teachers
actors.context: Characterized as being indoctrinated by education schools to prioritize self-esteem over academic skills.

actors.name: Sverdlov and Aram
actors.context: Researchers who documented the gap between parent and teacher expectations for kindergarten.

actors.name: Kail and Cavanaugh
actors.context: Authors of a widely used 2019 teacher training textbook analyzed by Hirsch for its emphasis on self-esteem over academic skills.

actors.name: Robert Putnam
actors.context: Cited to support the claim that individualism took over American elementary education in the mid-20th century.

actors.name: Harold Stevenson
actors.context: Cited for his research showing that developmental milestones like number sense differ between cultures.

actors.name: The National Commission on Excellence in Education
actors.context: The authors of 'A Nation at Risk' who Hirsch argues were university presidents and corporate chairs who misunderstood the root cause of the educational decline.

actors.name: Friedrich Froebel
actors.context: German inventor of the Kindergarten and proponent of developmentalism.

actors.name: John Dewey
actors.context: Key player in the adoption of developmentalism in American grade schools.

actors.name: Frederick Douglass
actors.context: Escaped slave who moved to Massachusetts due to its acceptance of black equality.

actors.name: Elizabeth Peabody
actors.context: Founder of the American kindergarten and disciple of Emerson.

actors.name: Friedrich Froebel
actors.context: German writer and inventor of the kindergarten.

actors.name: John Dewey
actors.context: Author of the 'Pedagogic Creed' and central figure in American educational philosophy.

actors.name: William Heard Kilpatrick
actors.context: Professor at Teachers College, Columbia University and author of 'The Project Method'.

actors.name: Teachers College, Columbia University
actors.context: The origin point of Kilpatrick's influential and, according to Hirsch, damaging 'Project Method' article.

actors.name: Schools of Education
actors.context: Institutions that Hirsch claims maintain developmentalism as a religious conviction and require students to learn from expensive developmental textbooks.

actors.name: Education professors
actors.context: Described as being 'devoted to developmentalism' and responsible for maintaining invalid theories in teacher training.

actors.name: Kail and Cavanaugh
actors.context: Authors of a teacher textbook Hirsch critiques for logical inconsistency regarding Piaget's theories.

actors.name: Susan Carey
actors.context: Cited for her work on 'The Origin of Concepts' supporting the idea that development is knowledge-bound.

actors.name: Education Schools
actors.context: Accused of indoctrinating teachers with the 'false theology' that the natural is inherently good and the artificial is bad.

actors.name: Developmentalists
actors.context: Criticized for using home disadvantage as an excuse to persist with ineffective, slow pedagogical methods.

actors.name: Susan Neuman
actors.context: Cited for her support of explicit systematic literacy instruction, though her 'discovery centers' are critiqued.

actors.name: Susan Carey
actors.context: Cited for research showing language proficiency determines educational attainment.

actors.name: PISA
actors.context: International ranking system cited to show the poor performance of the US educational system under developmentalism.

objections_raised.objection: Humans are born innocent of innate 'guilt' or corruption (the Pelagian view).
objections_raised.response: Observation of human behavior and modern sociobiology both confirm a 'doubleness' in human nature—an innate mixture of selfish and altruistic instincts.

objections_raised.objection: Directly teaching literacy and mathematics to kindergarteners is harmful to their proper psychological development.
objections_raised.response: Hirsch asserts this is 'not true' and cites developmental psychologists who show mental development is artificial and external rather than a fragile natural unfolding.

objections_raised.objection: Developmental textbooks are not entirely self-centered; they include references to altruism.
objections_raised.source: Kail and Cavanaugh (Human Development: A Life-Span View)
objections_raised.response: Hirsch notes that while altruism is mentioned (50 times), it is still overshadowed by self-esteem (97 times) and entirely ignores concepts like patriotism.

objections_raised.objection: Teacher-led classrooms are difficult to implement because current school furniture (hexagonal tables) is designed for child-centered peer interaction.
objections_raised.response: While the furniture is 'awkward' for teacher-led instruction, teachers and students who make the switch are 'highly enthusiastic' about the change despite the physical constraints.

objections_raised.objection: Piaget's theory remains the most complete account of cognitive development despite its flaws.
objections_raised.response: Hirsch argues this is a 'cavil' designed to please education professors who are devoted to developmentalism, even though the scientific consensus has moved on.

objections_raised.objection: Direct instruction is outdated, pedantic, and insufficiently considerate of student learning needs because it involves passive students sitting and taking notes.
objections_raised.response: Literacy is not an innate skill and has no biological blueprint; therefore, explicit 'active instruction from the outside' is the only way to achieve it.

objections_raised.objection: Natural education is inherently moral because nature is 'benign' or 'God’s providential agent.'
objections_raised.response: Nature is not a reliable guide to morality; without the 'curiosity of nations' (laws and customs), natural impulses can lead to destructive behavior (exemplified by Shakespeare's Edmund).

objections_raised.objection: Children should proceed at their own 'nature-determined pace.'
objections_raised.response: This is inconsistent with the fact that rich children 'develop' faster; if the pace is nature-determined, income should not change the speed. It also ignores the reality that schooling can overcome home-based slowness.

objections_raised.objection: Home 'disadvantage' in language and nutrition is an insurmountable barrier that justifies the slow progress of developmental education.
objections_raised.response: While these are grave disadvantages, effective, non-developmental schooling can and does overcome them by the early grades.

objections_raised.objection: Children should construct their own knowledge through discovery centers to foster exploration.
objections_raised.response: Discovery centers are a 'profligate waste of time' that are suboptimal compared to whole-class learning and disproportionately harm poor children who do not get knowledge at home.
